module_mood_contract: extension of grounded competence into capture-and-name (M2 second exercise) — student leaves with rule file at their location, sees automation horizon, deliberately defers building

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named; mood-floor 8+ on teaching beats.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block line 67: 'extension of grounded competence into capture-and-name.' Phases 1-3 mood-score 8; close 8; optional phase 4 mood-exempt (setup/student-agency carve-out per §1).
Maintainer Mood target reads as competence-extension, not corrective; push-back moves frame default-acceptance as a re-engagement opportunity, not as a wrong move.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Body opens with positive disciplines (capture-and-name, surface-the-rules, save where you'll find it again). Failure modes appear only in maintainer push-back section.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity — no front-running downstream teaching moments.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block 'Non-front-running checks' (lines 69-72) names M3 Ex3 (first skill-by-name), M5 (verifier-as-eval), M6 (eval-encoding) as reservations. Phase 2 explicitly saves a .md file, not a skill. Phase 3 names shapes, not invocations.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in module-specific language.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block line 73: four themes earned — capture-your-world (rule file IS the world; user-chosen location), compounding (observation→rule, written down), mirror (Claude reflects what you actually did), self-aware + grain of salt (Phase 3 introspection on automation paths).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Engagement steps (rewrite-and-reject pass, push-back if rule drifted, push for path proposal + @import line) are reachable inside the registry-expanded prompts {{prompt:extract-the-task-shaping-rule-1/2/3/4}}. Body sentence at line 18 names the pedagogical purpose without carrying the forcing function.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; no authored conversations or trainer cues.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No 'discuss with neighbor / share with the room / 90-seconds-with-the-person-next-to-you' patterns. Body sentences describe the WORK (ask Claude, save the file, read the answer).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Offered structures need a forced-engagement mechanic.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 1 forces rewrite-or-reject of at least one Claude-proposed rule (inside prompt registry entry); Phase 2 names a push-back step if Claude's three-rule restatement drifted from student wording. Default-acceptance failure mode is explicitly anticipated in maintainer push-backs (P1 default-acceptance line).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test — three observable Monday-morning outcomes.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block carries 'Mood target' (a feeling sentence) and 'Themes earned' (theme-instance list) but no dedicated three-named-verb leap-test section. Closest passage at line 67 is a feeling sentence — *'the rule sits in MY file at MY location; I see how this could automate; I'm not building it today'* — not three observable verbs another engineer would recognise as work.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a 'Leap test' block to the maintainer section naming three observable next-working-day verbs the student now exhibits — candidates: 'adds one rule born from a real planning session to their existing CLAUDE.local.md or ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md', 'names the loading model (auto-load vs lazy-load vs @import) when deciding where a rule belongs', 'deflects a build instinct toward capture-first when proposing automation against a backlog'. Author reconciles in /content-creation, not here.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch — every forcing-function phase names dominant failure + recovery.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer Push-back moves (lines 76-80) names five failure modes — P1 generic rules, P1 default-acceptance, P2 location-paralysis, P3 building-instinct, optional task-manager overreach — each with a paste-ready Nerd recovery line. Watch-fors section (lines 83-86) adds four diagnostic surfaces.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Pre-action framing doesn't preview the prompt's payoff.
rules_evaluated.evidence: '**What you do:**' (line 5) names ACTIVITY (read scrollback, ask Claude to surface rules, save .md, ask about automation, optionally reverse-engineer ticket) and ARTIFACT (.md file at chosen location). It does not name mechanism Claude will reveal or felt-evidence the engagement step will produce. '**The point:**' (line 7) names artifact-contract — acceptable per rule 53 artifact carve-out.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Host-skill optional; header reads 'Push-back moves' not 'Agentic Nerd push-backs'.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Header at line 75: '**Push-back moves** (trainer delivers by default; Nerd in self-study)' — exact compliance with host-varies framing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Context-loading tiers — auto-load + cross-session named at the choice point without front-running M3's tier reveal.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Body lines 22 + 27 name user-level (~/.claude/CLAUDE.md, auto-load) vs repo-personal (./CLAUDE.local.md, auto-load) vs notes-folder + @import. Free-choice keeps the M3 forthcoming auto-load / lazy-load / explicit-load tier reveal intact (maintainer line 72 names the reservation explicitly).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — close hands off to next module's open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer Arc section (line 97-98): hands off to lectures/where-the-rule-could-live.md, which closes the module on the leverage horizon without forcing a build. Capture-not-apply discipline holds at close and sets up M3 cleanly.
